title: Ensure that the --audit-log-maxage argument is set to 30 or as appropriate

input_row.title: Ensure that the --audit-log-maxage argument is set to 30 or as appropriate
input_row.description: Retain the logs for at least 30 days or as appropriate.
input_row.rationale: Retaining logs for at least 30 days ensures that you can go back in time and investigate or correlate any events. Set your audit log retention period to 30 days or as per your business requirements.
input_row.audit: Run the following command on the master node: ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver Verify that the --audit-log-maxage argument is set to 30 or as appropriate.
input_row.remediation: Edit the /etc/kubernetes/apiserver file on the master node and set the KUBE_API_ARGS parameter to "--audit-log-maxage=30" : KUBE_API_ARGS="--audit-log-maxage=30" Based on your system, restart the kube-apiserver service. For example: systemctl restart kube-apiserver.service Impact: None
input_row.default_value: By default, auditing is not enabled.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_api_server_audit_log_maxage_set

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the Kubernetes API to check the configuration of the kube-apiserver.
- The specific command to retrieve the kube-apiserver flags is:
  ```bash
  kubectl get pods -n kube-system -l component=kube-apiserver -o jsonpath='{.items[*].spec.containers[*].args}'
  ```
- This command will return the arguments passed to the kube-apiserver container, including the `--audit-log-maxage` flag.
- The expected value for `--audit-log-maxage` should be `30` or as appropriate based on organizational policy.
- Pass/Fail criteria:
  - **Pass:** If `--audit-log-maxage=30` is present in the output.
  - **Fail:** If `--audit-log-maxage` is not present or set to a value less than `30`.
